questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: The prohibition of gas-powered leaf blowers lasts for approximately 5 months, starting on October 1 each year and ending on the Friday commencing Memorial Day weekend of the following year.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: The permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation are as follows: Monday to Friday from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Saturdays and holidays from 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., and Sundays from 12:00 noon to 2:00 p.m.

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: The penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations include a fine not to exceed $250, imprisonment for up to 15 days, or both. Each day that a violation continues is considered a separate offense.
